title: centaurea boissieri subsp. lagascae
intro: Centaurea boissieri subsp. lagascae is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of North Africa. It is also known as Lagascale cornflower and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and improve overall health. It can be prepared into various herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to be used for the treatment of various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
